address: 306 Moo 2, Rim Kok, Muang Chiang Rai

description.en:
## Overview

The Blue Temple, officially Wat Rong Suea Ten, is a dazzling modern Buddhist temple about six kilometers north of Chiang Rai city center. Its striking sapphire-blue exterior and interior make it one of the most photogenic temples in all of Thailand. While it lacks the historical depth of older Lanna temples, its visual impact is extraordinary, and it has quickly become one of Chiang Rai's most visited attractions alongside the White Temple and Black House.

## History

The original temple on this site was ancient and had fallen into disrepair over the centuries. A complete redesign and reconstruction was undertaken by local artist Putha Kabkaew, a former student of Chalermchai Kositpipat, the creator of the famous White Temple. The newly completed Blue Temple was finished in 2016 and represents a contemporary interpretation of Thai Buddhist art, where the vivid blue color symbolizes wisdom and the purity of the Buddha's teachings. The name Rong Suea Ten translates roughly to "House of the Dancing Tiger," referring to a local legend about tigers that once roamed the area.

## What to See

The main prayer hall is breathtaking. Every surface is covered in deep blue paint adorned with intricate gold detailing, swirling patterns, and mythological figures. At the center sits a large white Buddha statue that contrasts dramatically against the blue and gold backdrop. The ceiling murals are particularly impressive, blending traditional Buddhist imagery with contemporary artistic techniques. Outside, the temple grounds feature blue-themed sculptures, naga serpent guardians, and smaller shrine buildings that continue the blue color scheme.

## Practical Info

The Blue Temple is open daily from 7:00 AM to 8:00 PM and entry is completely free, making it one of the best-value attractions in Chiang Rai. A standard dress code applies since it is an active place of worship: cover your shoulders and knees. The best times for photography are early morning from 7:00 to 9:00 AM or late afternoon from 4:00 to 6:00 PM when the light is softer and crowds are thinner. Getting here from central Chiang Rai costs about 100 to 150 THB by tuk-tuk or 80 to 120 THB by Grab. Most visitors spend 20 to 40 minutes here and combine the Blue Temple with the White Temple, Black House, and Wat Huay Pla Kang in a single day trip.
